directives a host page needs in order for the

adapters are wired in, then merges the result into its existing CSP
builder.

Options for enabling and configuring the Unsplash source provider.
DEV ONLY — ships the key to the browser; ignored when
; the package never bundles a key.
Throttle floor between JSON requests. Default 1200.
Opt-in re-host instead of hotlinking. Default false.

Per-request timeout for search / topic / photo lookups (ms). A request
that exceeds it aborts and surfaces as a retryable
error, so a blocked or flaky network path (e.g. a VPN/proxy black-holing

) lets the sidebar fall back to "Unsplash unavailable"
instead of spinning. Default 15_000. Ignored if not a positive number.

Parts already accepted by the backend (empty/omitted for a fresh session).

Effective part size in bytes, **locked for the life of the session**. The
runner slices every part at this size, so resuming MUST reuse the same
value — otherwise a skipped

would map to a different byte
range and corrupt the object. A backend may dictate it here; otherwise the
runner sets it from config and persists it (see

). Once parts have been uploaded it must
not change.

before

(so an interrupted upload can

finishes or is abandoned. Keyed internally by a stable file fingerprint
(name + size + lastModified), so the same picked file resumes while a
different file does not.

summary.text:
Create an in-memory asset registry.

The registry stores immutable

records by id and exposes the
local catalog operations used by the plugin runtime and Studio asset source:
registration, lookup, listing, delete, rename, replace, tag mutation, search,
pagination, and mutation subscriptions.
Create an IR asset resolver for

references.

The resolver looks up references in the supplied registry, re-validates the
stored URL at export/render time, and translates lookup or validation failures
into

codes for callers that need actionable export
diagnostics.
Adapt the upload registry into core's

. Synchronous (localStorage is
synchronous); the contract's optionally-async return types let custom hosts
back it with IndexedDB or a remote service.

URL. The result is
self-contained (no host backend) but ~33% larger than the raw bytes once
base64-encoded — see

Stable identity for an upload across reloads: the same file (by name, byte
length, and last-modified time) maps to the same key, while any edit changes
it.

delimiter can't be spoofed by a
filename.

This component wires the upload button, progress display, searchable asset
browser, command palette, delete/replace dialogs, and metadata editor around a
caller-provided registry and uploader. It is optional; headless integrations
can use the plugin runtime and registry APIs directly.

will reschedule the
underlying call rather than rethrow.

Adapters should throw

for HTTP 5xx, network
failures, and other recoverable conditions; non-retryable errors
(4xx, schema mismatches, abort) should be thrown as plain

overrides the next computed delay,
useful when the server returned a
Retry policy used by network-bound upload adapters.

Base delay in milliseconds. The actual delay grows exponentially
(
Cap on the computed backoff delay (before jitter).
Maximum number of retry attempts after the initial call.
Aborts both the in-flight call and any pending retry sleep.

between attempts and during sleep.

Resolution order on each error:
 1. If the signal is aborted → throw

immediately.
 2. If the error is not a

→ rethrow.
 3. If retries are exhausted → rethrow the last error.
 4. Otherwise compute backoff, sleep, and try again.
